title: Russian Forces Repel Massive Jihadist Offensive and Coup Attempt in Mali
summary: Russian-backed Africa Corps used helicopters and drones to crush a large-scale attack by Al-Qaeda-linked JNIM and Tuareg separatists targeting Bamako and key cities, thwarting a potential coup.
contents:
# Russian Forces Repel Massive Jihadist Offensive and Coup Attempt in Mali

## Overview
Russian-backed Africa Corps used helicopters and drones to crush a large-scale attack by Al-Qaeda-linked JNIM and Tuareg separatists targeting Bamako and key cities, thwarting a potential coup.

## Details
The offensive involved 10,000-12,000 fighters striking Bamako's international airport, presidential palace, Kati, Gao, Kidal, and Sévaré over the weekend. Mali's junta leader Assimi Goïta's defense minister Sadio Camara got killed in a suicide car bomb at his residence. Tuareg Azawad Liberation Front captured Kidal before Malian strikes hit back, while JNIM imposed a blockade on roads to Bamako. Russian forces claim the attack got West-backed, preserving the transitional government's authority.[1][2][3][4][5]

## Location
Bamako and northern Mali

## Source Context
Reporting referenced from coverage tracked by the @AfricaFactsZone OSINT beat. The cited outlet provides additional context, attribution, and verification of the claims summarized above.

## Tags
Mali, jihadists, JNIM, Tuareg rebels, Russia Africa Corps, coup attempt, Bamako attacks, Sahel security
